fundamentals.name: Cerebras Systems Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Cerebras Systems Inc. operates as an artificial intelligence infrastructure company. It designs and manufactures an AI compute platform that comprises of proprietary systems and software that is delivered in racks for deployment in data centers up to supercomputer scale. The company's pioneering wafer-scale engine, a chip encompassing a silicon wafer, designed to enable higher performance and speeds than GPUs for the computational demands of inference, Generative AI, and other AI applications. Its customers include hyperscalers, foundation model labs, AI-native and digital-native businesses, enterprises, and leaders of Sovereign AI initiatives. The company has operations in the United States, Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and internationally. Cerebras Systems Inc. was founded in 2015 and is headquartered in Sunnyvale, California.

judgment.summary: Extremely high growth company with significant operating losses and a very high valuation.
positives: Revenue growth 1474.4% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Profit margin 46.6% — very high, though likely due to accounting or non-recurring items given operating losses.
Return on equity 38.4% — strong on paper, but operating losses are a concern.
negatives: Trailing P/E 504.65, Price/Sales 93.44 — extremely high valuation multiples.
Operating margin -30.7% — the core business is unprofitable.
Free cash flow (TTM) $-622.92M — significant cash burn.
Debt/Equity 19.50 — high leverage relative to equity.
Return on assets -5.3% — negative returns on company assets.

news.paragraph: Cerebras Systems (CBRS) has experienced significant volatility since its IPO, with shares plunging nearly 70% from their peak and currently down 24% from the opening day close. Despite this sharp decline, a Yahoo Finance article suggests a trend launched by Cerebras could supercharge the AI bull market, while insider transactions were also reported. The company's recent IPO performance has been a mixed bag, soaring initially before facing a significant downturn.

examples.body: $CBRS Added one more tranche at the $216 break. Whenever this turns, it oughta happen in huge chunks IMHO. Could easily retake $275-300 in literally a few days…
$CBRS love this. Walking the shorts right into the trap. Keep going lower. I’ll keep picking away, to me this is a long term hold that will turn up at the end …
$CBRS First toe dip just filled… but I see us getting into the low teens too
$CBRS start nibbling below $200, preferably around its IPO price of $190. Still has room to drop.
$CBRS Insiders cannot sell, so the retailers are selling at lower price than what they bought at. Fear can bring such reaction. ￼
$CBRS Love it when it gets cheaper by the day.. buying at IPO is done by those who hate money
